facts.text: Rye Brook village grew from land bought from Native Americans in 1640, marking the latest chapter in the Town of Rye's long history.
Rye is home to Westchester County's only National Historic Landmark District, which includes the Jay Estate where Founding Father John Jay once lived.
The American Yacht Club, founded in 1883, still hosts sailing and tennis on Rye's waterfront, a tradition that began over a century ago.
Rye's waterfront also contains the Boston Post Road Historic District, the first National Historic Landmark in the area, designated in 1993.
The village's name probably honors Rye in Sussex, England, reflecting settlers' origins, though the exact reason remains unclear.
Rye secured a $3 million grant to safeguard historic buildings and wetlands from flooding, underscoring its modern preservation efforts.
